_note: Every externally-sourced claim shown on the site. Rule: a number appears publicly only if it comes from company documents or carries a named external source below. accessed = date the source was checked.

claims.claim: 112.2 million adults and juniors play golf worldwide (excluding USA & Mexico)
claims.source: The R&A — Global Golf Participation 2025

claims.claim: 47.1 million juniors play golf globally (excl. US/Mexico), up 3.2M in one year
claims.source: The R&A — Global Golf Participation 2025

claims.claim: 48.1 million Americans age 6+ played golf on- or off-course in 2025
claims.source: National Golf Foundation — Golf Participation in the U.S. 2026

claims.claim: 29.1 million Americans played on a golf course in 2025 — eighth consecutive year of growth
claims.source: National Golf Foundation — Graffis Report 2025 / Participation 2026

claims.claim: US golf participation is on track to eclipse 50 million during 2026
claims.source: National Golf Foundation — The Road to 50 Million Golfers

claims.claim: Great Britain rounds played rose 14% in 2025 — the highest total since comparable records began in 2005
claims.source: Golf Today / The Golf Business (Sports Marketing Surveys data)

claims.claim: England alone has 1,735 golf clubs and 730,000+ club members
claims.source: The Golf Business (England Golf data)

claims.claim: Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman, Qatar and Bahrain are investing heavily in golf infrastructure; golf is a named pillar of Saudi Vision 2030 sports tourism
claims.source: Golf Digest Middle East; Vision 2030 sports tourism programme

claims.claim: Global golf tourism is a ~$28bn market (2025), projected to reach ~$62bn by 2034
claims.source: Fortune Business Insights — Golf Tourism Market

claims.claim: More than 12 million Americans traveled to play golf in each year 2022–2025
claims.source: National Golf Foundation — Travel's Expanding Golf Economy Impact

claims.claim: Golfbreaks sends ~220,000 golfers on trips every year and has sent 3.5M+ golfers away to date
claims.source: Golfbreaks / GolfWRX profile

claims.claim: Your Golf Travel is the world's largest golf travel company, covering 3,500+ destinations
claims.source: Your Golf Travel

claims.claim: GolfZon operates 6,500+ commercial simulator locations in South Korea and holds ~60% of the global commercial simulator market
claims.source: Forbes / Seoulz — Korea Screen Golf Industry 2026

claims.claim: ~94 million rounds were logged on GolfZon simulators in South Korea in a single year — nearly twice the traditional rounds played nationwide
claims.source: GOLF.com — Inside South Korea's obsessive love of the game

claims.claim: Korean screen golf is a $1.6bn industry across ~9,000 domestic venues
claims.source: Seoulz — Korea Screen Golf Industry 2026

claims.claim: The global golf simulator market reached ~$2.4bn in 2025, projected to ~$4.8bn by 2035
claims.source: InsightAce Analytic / Grand View Research

claims.claim: 19 million Americans participated exclusively in off-course golf (simulators, ranges, golf entertainment) in 2025
claims.source: National Golf Foundation — Golf Participation in the U.S. 2026

claims.claim: The odds of an amateur making a hole-in-one are ~1 in 12,500; roughly one ace occurs per 3,500 rounds; only 1–2% of golfers make one in a given year
claims.source: National Hole-in-One Registry via The Hustle

claims.claim: Hole-in-one insurance is an established prize-indemnity niche — a $220m market by the 1990s with 24+ specialist firms; Get Lucky consumerizes it
claims.source: The Hustle — The strange business of hole-in-one insurance

claims.claim: Prize-indemnity / hole-in-one insurance is a $1.2bn market today, projected to reach $2.4bn by 2033 (8.1% CAGR)
claims.source: Company research — prize-indemnity market reports (not independently verified)

claims.claim: There are 38,000+ golf courses across 206 of the world's 251 countries
claims.source: The R&A — Golf Around the World (via National Golf Foundation)

claims.claim: Course counts in key European markets: UK 3,101, Germany 1,050, France 804
claims.source: The R&A — Golf Around the World

claims.claim: Ernie Els: 4 major championships (US Open 1994, 1997; The Open 2002, 2012), 70+ worldwide professional wins, former world No. 1, 788 weeks in the world top 10, World Golf Hall of Fame (2011), record 7× World Match Play champion
claims.source: Wikipedia / ernieels.com / European Tour records

claims.claim: Ernie Els' commercial network spans the exact markets Get Lucky is expanding into: The Els Club Dubai, Els Club Teluk Datai & Desaru Coast (Malaysia), Trump International Lido (Indonesia), Ernie Els Design with an Asia-Pacific office, and Ernie Els Wines distributed in Asia travel retail
claims.value: UK / US / Middle East / Asia footprint
claims.source: ernieels.com — Course Portfolio & About Ernie Els Design
